checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only text found in the source document or expected AI-generated elements like alt text for images. No fabricated summaries, titles, or body text were identified.

All contact emails (DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us, anna.verrill@nc.gov, tim.johnson@nc.gov) found on the last slide of the source document are accurately reflected in the output JSON. No fabricated phone numbers or URLs were found.
All 16 slides from the source document are accounted for in the JSON structure. While several slides (11-16) are grouped into a single section, their content is preserved sequentially.
The source document does not contain any tables; data is presented via charts (images) and lists. This check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The textual content within each section correctly reflects the content on the corresponding slides. No significant paragraphs or list items were omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor differences in text formatting, such as treating 'th' in 'March 11th' as a superscript element, are acceptable and do not alter the meaning. The information provided in the JSON matches the source document faithfully.

checks.a_analysis: The output document correctly represents the content of the 16-page source PDF without duplicating text blocks. Each page's content is present exactly once, with pages 11 through 16 being consolidated into a single section (Section 11), which is an acceptable structural transformation.

The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically structured. The document title is rendered as an H2, and subsequent major questions or section titles are treated either as section titles or H3 elements. While there is minor inconsistency in whether a title is a section property or an explicit heading element, the overall hierarchy is flat and appropriate for a slide-based document converted to structural JSON. No heading levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structure corruption occurred.
The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the original PDF slides from page 1 to page 16.

Lists are converted correctly. Page 2 features a nested list structure (Background -> drafted questions -> specific data types) which is accurately represented in the JSON with `nested_list` objects. Other bulleted lists on pages 3, 7, 9, 11, 12, 14, and 15 are properly captured as unordered list items.

The contact emails on page 16 are correctly identified, and the pipeline has appropriately converted the NC.gov email addresses into clickable 'mailto:' links. No broken or fabricated links were observed.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly maintains the sequential flow of the original presentation slides. Content from the sidebar and individual slides is presented in a logical, single-column vertical layout. While styling such as fonts, colors, and the slide-based structure has changed to a continuous document format, the visual organization of information is preserved and the document's purpose remains clear. The conversion from a landscape slide deck to a responsive HTML layout is handled appropriately.

The source document does not contain traditional grid-style tables. Data presented in multi-column lists (e.g., page 11) has been converted into standard bulleted lists in the HTML, which is an appropriate and readable representation for the format.

The HTML output fails to render several critical meaningful images from the source PDF. Specifically, the horizontal bar charts on slides Q1 (page 4), Q2 (page 5), Q3 (page 6), and Q5 (page 8), and the pie chart on slide Q7 (page 10), are either missing or replaced by solid green rectangular placeholders. These charts contain the primary data results of the survey, and their absence results in a significant loss of information for a sighted user.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual charts (bar charts and pie charts) on PDF pages 4, 5, 6, 8, and 10 representing survey data results.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows solid green blocks or missing content under sections Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7 where the charts should be.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7 in the HTML output

checks.a_analysis: Several sections in the HTML output exhibit visual duplication of heading text within the body of the page (not counting the Table of Contents). The slide titles from the PDF are rendered twice consecutively: once as a structured heading and immediately again as a paragraph of plain text. This duplication is evident in sections Q3, Q5, Q7, Q8, and most notably Q10, where the entire long text block is repeated.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 10 heading: "Q7: Are any of your operational costs associated with the population of your jurisdiction? (For example, software licensing fees)"
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML renders the question text twice: "Q7: Are any of your operational costs associated with the population of your jurisdiction? (For example, software licensing fees)" (Heading) followed by "Q7: Are any of your operational costs associated with the population of your jurisdiction? (For example, software licensing fees)" (Body text).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections Q3, Q5, Q7, Q8, and Q10 in the HTML output
